title: Set Up the Observer Agent
lede: Run a second AI in the background that watches every live call, flags important moments as events, and can silently trigger tools - without slowing the main conversation.

steps.title: Enable the Observer Agent
steps.content:
1. Go to Intelligence Center → Smart Agents and open your agent.
  2. Open the Observer Agent panel and turn it on (it is off by default).
  3. The panel has two areas: Configuration and Events.

images.caption: The Observer Agent panel with Configuration and Events.
The Observer Agent panel with Configuration and Events.

steps.title: Configure the Model and Tools
steps.content:
1. Under Configuration, pick an LLM Model to power the observer. Because it runs in the background, you can use a slower, more thorough model without affecting call latency.
  2. Optionally choose an MCP Server so the observer can trigger background tools when conditions are met - without making the main agent wait.

images.caption: The observer runs alongside the main agent during every call.
The observer runs alongside the main agent during every call.

steps.title: Set Up Events
steps.content:
• Open Events to see the defaults, including Call Quality Issues, Customer Frustration Detected, Escalation Request, Payment Information Discussed, High-Value Opportunity, and more.
  • Click Add Custom Event to create your own with a name, description, and color. You can disable events you don't need or use Reset to Defaults.

steps.title: Watch It on Live Calls
steps.content:
1. Open the Live Calls screen while a call is active.
  2. When the observer detects an event (for example the caller says "I can't hear you"), the call is tagged and moved to the matching column.
  3. Filter by event to focus on the conversations that need attention.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
